studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: The intervention period is 90 days, and primary and secondary outcomes are measured at day 90.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group receives the DiGA companion® shoulder for 90 days, including personalized exercise therapy and education. Control group receives standard physiotherapy care for 90 days, typically 1 to 3 prescriptions with 6 units each.
studies.collected_data: Primary outcome: Shoulder-specific pain reduction measured with Numeric Pain Rating Scale (NPRS) after 90 days. Secondary outcomes: Shoulder-specific functional improvement measured with Shoulder Pain and Disability Index (SPADI) after 90 days, and number of days of incapacity for work due to illness within the survey period measured via self-reporting at day 90.

audit.study_title: Multicenter randomized controlled trial (RCT) to demonstrate the positive care effects of the medical device companion® shoulder in adult patients with conservatively managed shoulder lesions with subacromial pain
audit.note: registry-only: outcome measures captured under collected_data; no reported results
